title: Explain Backpropagation Algorithm
description: Define backpropagation and explain how gradients flow backward through a neural network. Describe the chain rule application for computing partial derivatives with respect to each weight parameter.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive to explain backprop mechanics (chain rule, gradient flow), then Technical Tutorial Development to structure the explanation clearly.

title: Compare SGD vs Adam Optimizer
description: Compare Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) and Adam optimizers in terms of convergence speed, memory requirements, and typical use cases. When would you choose one over the other?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis to structure the SGD vs Adam comparison, with Trade-off Analysis for convergence/memory/use-case dimensions.

title: Explain Attention Mechanism
description: Explain the scaled dot-product attention mechanism used in Transformers. Describe what queries, keys, and values represent and how attention weights are computed.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for scaled dot-product attention math, supplemented by Formal Definition for Q/K/V notation.

title: Define CNN Architecture
description: Define the basic architecture of a Convolutional Neural Network. Explain convolutional layers, pooling, and how they extract spatial features from image data.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog to describe CNN layers, with Architecture Diagram Design to visually map layer interactions and data flow.

title: Explain Regularization Techniques
description: Define L1 and L2 regularization. Explain how they prevent overfitting by penalizing large weights and what the geometric difference is between them.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for L1/L2 penalty mechanics, Formal Definition for the geometric interpretation.

title: Understanding Loss Functions
description: Explain three common loss functions: cross-entropy, mean squared error, and hinge loss. Describe when each is appropriate and what objective they optimize.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis to compare three loss functions, Formal Definition for mathematical objectives.

title: RNN vs LSTM Comparison
description: Compare Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. Explain the vanishing gradient problem and how LSTMs address it.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for RNN vs LSTM, Algorithm Deep Dive for vanishing gradient and gating mechanisms.

title: Batch Normalization Overview
description: Explain batch normalization: what problem does it solve, how does it normalize inputs, and what are the benefits for training speed and stability?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for batch norm mechanics, Analogy-Based Explanation to make the normalization intuition clear.

title: What is Transfer Learning
description: Define transfer learning and explain why pre-training on large datasets then fine-tuning on task-specific data is effective. Provide one concrete example.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for transfer learning theory, Technical Blog Post Writing for accessible practitioner-oriented explanation with concrete example.

title: Few-Shot Learning Basics
description: Explain few-shot learning: what is it, how does it differ from traditional supervised learning, and name two approaches (e.g., meta-learning, in-context learning).
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for few-shot learning concepts, Comparative Technical Analysis to contrast meta-learning vs in-context learning.

title: Dropout Regularization Mechanism
description: Explain how dropout prevents overfitting during training. Describe the dropout probability parameter and how it affects network training vs inference.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for dropout mechanics (training vs inference), Analogy-Based Explanation for intuitive understanding.

title: Neural Architecture Search Intro
description: Briefly explain Neural Architecture Search (NAS): what problem does it solve, what are the main search strategies (e.g., reinforcement learning, evolutionary), and computational cost considerations.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for NAS search strategies, Scalability and Complexity Analysis for computational cost considerations.

title: Gradient Descent Variants
description: Compare Batch GD, Stochastic GD, and Mini-batch GD in terms of computational cost, noise level, and convergence properties.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for batch/stochastic/mini-batch comparison, Algorithm Deep Dive for convergence properties.

title: Activation Functions in Deep Learning
description: Name and explain four activation functions (ReLU, Sigmoid, Tanh, SoftMax). Describe when each is used and advantages/disadvantages.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for activation function comparison, Example Generation for use-case examples.

title: Overfitting and Underfitting
description: Define overfitting and underfitting. Explain the bias-variance tradeoff and strategies to diagnose and mitigate each.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for bias-variance tradeoff, Abstraction Ladder to connect low-level overfitting symptoms to high-level modeling concepts.

title: Normalization Techniques Overview
description: Compare batch normalization, layer normalization, and instance normalization. For each technique, explain the axis of normalization, typical use cases (CNNs vs RNNs vs style transfer), and how the choice affects training stability and convergence speed. When would you choose one over the others?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for batch/layer/instance norm comparison, Algorithm Deep Dive for normalization axis mechanics.

title: Initialization Strategies
description: Explain why proper weight initialization matters in deep networks. Describe Xavier/Glorot and He initialization and why they prevent vanishing/exploding gradients.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for Xavier/He initialization derivation, Formal Definition for mathematical foundations.

title: Classification vs Regression
description: Define classification and regression problems. Name one loss function and one metric appropriate for each.
expected_approach: Use Formal Definition to define classification vs regression precisely, Example Generation for loss/metric examples.

title: Cross-Validation for Model Selection
description: Explain k-fold cross-validation: why it's better than a single train-test split for hyperparameter tuning and how it estimates generalization error.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for k-fold mechanics, Metric Selection for generalization error estimation.

title: Embedding Layers in Neural Networks
description: Explain what embedding layers do, why they're useful for categorical data, and how they reduce dimensionality while capturing semantic relationships.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for embedding layer mechanics, Analogy-Based Explanation for semantic relationship intuition.

title: Advanced Backpropagation Analysis
description: You have a 4-layer feedforward network with ReLU activations and squared error loss. The gradient for weight w2 in layer 2 is computed as ∂L/∂w2 = (∂L/∂y)(∂y/∂o3)(∂o3/∂o2)(∂o2/∂w2). Explain why certain intermediate terms might vanish and how residual connections or skip connections mitigate this.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for gradient chain derivation, Failure Mode Analysis for vanishing gradient diagnosis and residual connection mitigation.

title: Design Optimizer for Sparse Data
description: You need to optimize a model for a task with sparse feature vectors and infrequent updates to different parameters. Compare why AdaGrad or Proximal SGD might be better than vanilla Adam for this scenario. What does each do to handle sparsity?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for AdaGrad vs Proximal SGD vs Adam, Trade-off Analysis for sparsity handling mechanisms.

title: Multi-Head Attention Deep Dive
description: A Transformer layer uses 8 attention heads. Explain why multiple heads help capture different relationships. Design an experiment to measure if all heads learn distinct patterns or if some are redundant.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for multi-head attention mechanics, Ablation Study Design for the redundancy experiment.

title: Analyzing CNN Filter Learning
description: After training a CNN on 100,000 images, you notice that many filters in early layers look similar (e.g., edge detectors). Is this redundancy a problem? Explain why it happens and whether you should prune similar filters or use regularization to encourage diversity.
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis to analyze filter redundancy, Problem Reformulation to reframe whether redundancy is a bug or a feature.

title: Loss Function Landscape Analysis
description: Compare cross-entropy loss vs focal loss for imbalanced classification. Given a dataset where one class is 95% of data, explain how focal loss reshapes the landscape to focus on hard examples. Derive or explain the mathematical difference.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for focal loss derivation and comparison, Formal Definition for mathematical difference.

title: Dropout Rate Optimization
description: You're training a model and observing underfitting with dropout=0.2 but overfitting with dropout=0.5. Explain what's happening and design a principled approach to select the optimal dropout rate given validation data.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for dropout rate effects, A/B Test Design for principled hyperparameter selection with validation.

title: Meta-Learning vs Transfer Learning
description: Compare meta-learning (learning to learn) and transfer learning. When would you use MAML (Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning) over fine-tuning a pre-trained model? Provide a concrete scenario.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for meta-learning vs transfer learning, Example Generation for concrete MAML scenario.

title: NAS Search Space Design
description: You're designing a search space for NAS to find optimal architectures for medical image segmentation. What architectural choices would you include (e.g., layers, widths, skip connections)? How would you constrain the search to keep computational cost reasonable?
expected_approach: Use Design Space Exploration for NAS search space definition, Scalability and Complexity Analysis for compute constraints.

title: Gradient Checkpointing Trade-offs
description: Gradient checkpointing trades memory for computation time by recomputing activations during backward pass. Analyze when this trade-off is worth it. How does it affect training speed for different batch sizes?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for memory vs computation trade-off, Scalability and Complexity Analysis for batch size interactions.

title: Mixed Precision Training
description: Explain mixed precision training: why use FP16 for activations but FP32 for weights? Describe loss scaling and why it's necessary. What are the speedups vs numerical stability risks?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for FP16/FP32 and loss scaling mechanics, Trade-off Analysis for speedup vs stability risks.

title: Curriculum Learning Strategy
description: Design a curriculum learning strategy for training a vision model on images of varying complexity (simple geometric shapes → complex real-world scenes). Explain how to order examples and adapt difficulty progression.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for curriculum ordering strategy, Scenario Planning for difficulty progression adaptation.

title: Knowledge Distillation Pipeline
description: You have a large teacher model (1B params) and want to distill it to a small student (50M params) for deployment. Design the distillation process: what is the student trained to mimic (hard targets, soft targets, both)? How do you weight the two objectives?
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for distillation process, Algorithm Deep Dive for hard/soft target mechanics and objective weighting.

title: Adversarial Training Analysis
description: Explain adversarial training for robustness: why it makes models more robust but sometimes hurts clean accuracy. Design an experiment to measure the clean vs robust accuracy frontier.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for adversarial training mechanics, A/B Test Design for clean vs robust accuracy frontier experiment.

title: Explainability via Attention Visualization
description: Using a Transformer model, you want to explain which tokens the model attended to when making a prediction. Design a visualization and analysis strategy to interpret attention patterns. How do you distinguish genuine reasoning from noise?
expected_approach: Use Visualization Design for attention pattern visualization, Assumption Surfacing to distinguish genuine reasoning from noise.

title: Data Augmentation Strategy Design
description: For a medical imaging task (X-rays), design a comprehensive data augmentation pipeline. Which augmentations are safe (preserve diagnostic information) vs risky? How do you validate that augmentation doesn't introduce bias?
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for augmentation pipeline, Bias Detection for validating augmentation doesn't introduce bias.

title: Hyperparameter Interaction Effects
description: Learning rate and batch size have complex interactions (you can often increase LR proportionally to batch size). Explain this interaction theoretically (noise scale, gradient magnitude) and design an experiment to find the optimal (batch_size, lr) pairs.
expected_approach: Use A/B Test Design for the (batch_size, lr) experiment, Configuration Management for systematic hyperparameter exploration.

title: Continual Learning Without Catastrophic Forgetting
description: A model is trained on Task A, then fine-tuned on Task B. It forgets Task A performance (catastrophic forgetting). Name two approaches to prevent this (e.g., elastic weight consolidation, replay buffers) and compare their computational/memory costs.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for EWC vs replay buffers, Algorithm Deep Dive for mechanisms and computational costs.

title: Model Pruning vs Quantization
description: Compare weight pruning (removing small weights) and quantization (lower bit precision) for model compression. Which is better for inference latency vs accuracy? Can you combine them?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for pruning vs quantization, Trade-off Analysis for latency vs accuracy and combination strategies.

title: Ensemble Methods Beyond Voting
description: Simple ensemble voting often improves accuracy. Design a more sophisticated ensemble strategy: how would you weight models, combine predictions for regression, or use stacking to train a meta-model?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for stacking/meta-model mechanics, Design Space Exploration for sophisticated ensemble strategies.

title: Class Imbalance Mitigation Comparative Analysis
description: A classification task has 1% positive examples. Compare oversampling, undersampling, weighted loss, threshold adjustment, and cost-sensitive learning. For each, explain the bias-variance implications and computational cost.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis across 5 imbalance methods, Trade-off Analysis for bias-variance and computational implications.

title: System Design: Image Classification Pipeline
description: Design a complete image classification system from scratch for a startup. Include: data preprocessing, model architecture choice, training strategy (single vs ensemble), deployment considerations (latency, memory), and monitoring for concept drift. Justify each design decision.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for system architecture, Pipeline Design for end-to-end pipeline, Architecture Diagram Design for visual system documentation.

title: Scaling Laws and Compute Optimal Architecture Search
description: Recent work (Kaplan et al., Hoffmann et al.) shows that model size, training tokens, and compute budget follow power laws. Given a fixed compute budget C, how do you allocate between model parameters N and training tokens T? Analyze the Chinchilla scaling laws and design optimal model configurations.
expected_approach: Use Systematic Literature Review for Kaplan/Hoffmann papers, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for scaling law results, Algorithm Deep Dive for Chinchilla analysis.

title: Generalization Bounds and Empirical Risk Minimization
description: Derive or explain VC dimension, Rademacher complexity, or PAC learning bounds. Given a training set of size n, how do these inform what test error you can expect? How do regularization parameters (λ) relate to these generalization bounds?
expected_approach: Use Formal Definition for VC dimension/PAC learning bounds, Algorithm Deep Dive for regularization-generalization connection.

title: Designing Federated Learning System
description: You're building a federated learning system where models train on edge devices without centralizing data. Design the communication protocol, aggregation strategy (FedAvg vs FedProx), and robustness against client dropout or adversarial clients.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for federated learning design, Pipeline Design for communication protocol, Scalability Analysis for client management.

title: Causal Inference in Deep Learning
description: Explain the difference between correlation and causation in ML. Design an experiment to distinguish correlation-based learning from causal learning using interventional data or causal graphs. How would you test if a model captures causal relationships?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for causal inference theory, A/B Test Design for interventional experiment, Formal Definition for causal graph notation.

title: Interpretable ML System Design
description: For a high-stakes decision system (e.g., loan approval), design a ML approach that is both accurate and interpretable. Compare rule-based systems, decision trees, linear models, and attention-based neural networks. How do you balance accuracy vs interpretability?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for system design, Trade-off Analysis for accuracy vs interpretability, Comparative Analysis for model types.

title: Benchmark Gaming and Robust Evaluation
description: Many models achieve high accuracy on standard benchmarks but fail in deployment. Analyze why (benchmark saturation, distribution shift, adversarial examples, spurious correlations). Design a robust evaluation protocol that better predicts real-world performance.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for robust evaluation, Failure Mode Analysis for benchmark gaming diagnosis, Gap Analysis for identifying what benchmarks miss vs real-world.

title: Multi-Task Learning Architecture Design
description: Design a multi-task learning system that learns 5 related tasks simultaneously. How do you handle conflicting gradients between tasks? Compare hard parameter sharing, soft parameter sharing, and task-specific adapters. When is MTL better than single-task?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for MTL system design, Algorithm Deep Dive for conflicting gradient handling, Trade-off Analysis for sharing strategies.

title: Automating Hyperparameter Tuning
description: Compare random search, grid search, Bayesian optimization, and evolutionary algorithms for hyperparameter tuning. Design a meta-learning approach that predicts good hyperparameters for new datasets based on dataset characteristics.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for search methods, Design Space Exploration for meta-learning approach, Algorithm Deep Dive for Bayesian optimization.

title: Foundation Model Adaptation Framework
description: You have access to a large pre-trained foundation model. Design a framework for efficiently adapting it to 100 different downstream tasks with limited labeled data per task. Include parameter-efficient methods (LoRA, adapters), transfer learning strategies, and multi-task optimization.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for adaptation framework, Conceptual Framework Building for unifying LoRA/adapters/multi-task concepts, Algorithm Deep Dive for parameter-efficient mechanics.

title: Explain ReAct Framework
description: Describe the ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) framework. Explain how interleaving reasoning steps with actions helps agents solve complex tasks. Provide one example prompt that demonstrates the ReAct pattern.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for ReAct framework components, Example Generation for a demonstration prompt.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
description: Explain chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting and why it improves model performance on reasoning tasks. How does it differ from direct prompting? Give a concrete example of a problem where CoT helps.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for CoT mechanics, Example Generation for concrete CoT demonstration.

title: Tool Use in Language Models
description: Define tool use for language models: what tools might an agent use, how does it decide when to call a tool, and how does it interpret results? Name three example tools.
expected_approach: Use Taxonomy and Classification to categorize tool types, Example Generation for three tool examples.

title: AutoGPT Agent Components
description: Describe the key components of an AutoGPT-style agent: memory, planning, task decomposition, and execution. How does it iteratively refine tasks and handle failures?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for AutoGPT components, Taxonomy to classify memory/planning/execution subsystems.

title: Planning Algorithms for Agents
description: Compare breadth-first search, depth-first search, and A* for agent planning. When would each be appropriate? How do you define heuristics for A*?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for BFS/DFS/A*, Algorithm Deep Dive for heuristic definition.

title: Memory Systems in Agents
description: Explain three types of memory for agents: episodic (what happened), semantic (facts and concepts), and procedural (skills). How should an agent use each type when solving problems?
expected_approach: Use Taxonomy for episodic/semantic/procedural classification, Concept Map Construction to map relationships between memory types and agent behaviors.

title: Reflection and Self-Improvement
description: What is reflection in agents? How can an agent review its past actions, identify mistakes, and improve future behavior? Provide one concrete implementation approach.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for reflection mechanisms, Example Generation for concrete implementation.

title: Function Calling and API Integration
description: Explain function calling: how an LLM decides what function to call, with what arguments, and how it handles the return value. Describe the JSON schema format commonly used.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for function calling flow, Formal Definition for JSON schema format.

title: Prompt Chaining Patterns
description: Define prompt chaining: sequencing multiple LLM calls where output from one becomes input to the next. Give two examples of chaining patterns and explain why it helps complex tasks.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for chaining patterns, Example Generation for two concrete examples.

title: Error Handling in Agents
description: Describe strategies for error handling in LLM agents: detecting errors, recovering from failures, retrying with modified prompts. What's a principled approach to error recovery?
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for error types, System Debugging for recovery strategies.

title: Agent Evaluation Metrics
description: What metrics would you use to evaluate an agent? Consider task success rate, step efficiency, cost, safety, and robustness. How would you measure each?
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for agent evaluation metrics, Evaluation Rubric Design for measurement criteria.

title: Tree-of-Thought vs Chain-of-Thought
description: Compare chain-of-thought (single path reasoning) with tree-of-thought (exploring multiple reasoning paths). When is branching exploration better? How do you manage the exponential cost?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for CoT vs ToT, Scalability and Complexity for exponential cost management.

title: Grounding LLM Outputs
description: Explain why grounding (connecting LLM outputs to facts, tools, external knowledge) is important for agents. Name three grounding mechanisms and when each is useful.
expected_approach: Use Taxonomy for grounding mechanism types, Example Generation for concrete mechanism examples.

title: Agent State Management
description: How should an agent maintain state across multiple interactions? What should be stored (context, decisions, outcomes)? How do you balance completeness with context length limitations?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for state management design, Trade-off Analysis for completeness vs context limits.

title: Uncertainty Quantification in Agents
description: How can an agent quantify uncertainty in its decisions? What mechanisms allow it to express confidence, request clarification, or defer to humans?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for uncertainty quantification methods, Metric Selection for confidence expression.

title: Advanced ReAct Analysis
description: The basic ReAct loop is: Thought → Action → Observation → repeat. Analyze failure modes: when does an agent get stuck in loops? How would you detect and break infinite loops? Design a mechanism to force progression (e.g., limiting repeated thoughts).
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for ReAct loop failures, System Debugging Protocol for infinite loop detection and breaking.

title: Combining CoT with Tool Use
description: Design an agent that interleaves reasoning (CoT) with tool calls. When should the agent reason before calling a tool vs calling immediately? How do you prevent excessive reasoning or unnecessary tool calls?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for reasoning vs tool-calling balance, Architecture Pattern Catalog for interleaving design.

title: Hierarchical Task Decomposition
description: An agent faces a complex goal: 'Write a research paper.' Design a hierarchical decomposition: top-level tasks, subtasks, and atomic actions. How does the agent decide which level to work at?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for hierarchical decomposition, Pipeline Design for task/subtask/atomic action levels.

title: Memory Optimization for Long Tasks
description: An agent works on a task for 50 steps, accumulating 50k tokens of context. Context windows are limited to 8k tokens. Design a memory summarization strategy: what to keep verbatim vs summarize vs discard?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for verbatim vs summarize vs discard decisions, Pipeline Design for memory management strategy.

title: Reflection Mechanism Design
description: Design a reflection step for agents: how often should an agent reflect? What should it review (recent actions, success/failure patterns, goal progress)? How does reflection output modify future behavior?
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for reflection criteria, Architecture Pattern Catalog for reflection mechanism structure.

title: Function Specification and Validation
description: An agent has access to 20 functions with complex signatures. Design a specification format (JSON schema, docstrings) that lets the agent understand what each does. How do you validate that the agent calls functions correctly?
expected_approach: Use Formal Definition for function specification format, Testing Strategy for validation approach.

title: Prompt Chaining with Branching
description: Design a system for chained prompts where some chains branch conditionally: 'If X, then do A-B-C, else do D-E.' How do you manage state across branches and merge results?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for branching chain design, Pipeline Design for state management across branches.

title: Robust Error Recovery Strategy
description: An agent encounters a parsing error from a tool (malformed JSON). Design a recovery strategy: retry with reformatted input, use a fallback tool, or ask for clarification. How do you prevent infinite error loops?
expected_approach: Use System Debugging for recovery strategy, Failure Mode Analysis for error loop prevention.

title: Multi-Agent Coordination Patterns
description: You want two agents to collaborate: Agent A focuses on research, Agent B on synthesis. Design a communication protocol: how do they share findings, resolve conflicts, and coordinate work?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for multi-agent communication, Pipeline Design for coordination protocol.

title: Cost Control in Agentic Systems
description: An agentic system costs $1 per task if it works in 10 steps but $10 if it takes 100 steps. Design a cost-aware agent that trades accuracy for speed. How do you balance quality vs cost?
expected_approach: Use Resource Estimation for cost modeling, Trade-off Analysis for quality vs cost balance.

title: Safety and Alignment in Agents
description: Design safety guardrails for an agent system: preventing harmful actions, misuse of tools, and goal misalignment. What constraints, monitors, and overrides would you implement?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for guardrail design, Monitoring and Observability for safety monitors.

title: Agent Behavior Logging and Debugging
description: An agent fails on 10% of tasks silently (produces wrong output without error). Design a logging and debugging system: what to log, how to identify failure modes, and how to trace the root cause?
expected_approach: Use System Debugging for failure investigation, Monitoring and Observability for logging system design.

title: Scaling Agents to Many Tasks
description: You need to deploy agents to handle 1,000 concurrent tasks. Design the infrastructure: task queue, agent pool management, result aggregation. How do you handle rate limiting and resource contention?
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for task queue and agent pool, Resource Estimation for capacity planning.

title: Dynamic Tool Selection
description: An agent has access to 30 specialized tools but using wrong tool wastes tokens. Design a mechanism for the agent to select appropriate tools: tool recommendation, cost-benefit analysis, or meta-reasoning about tool applicability?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for tool cost-benefit, Architecture Pattern Catalog for dynamic selection mechanism.

title: Learning from Agent Failures
description: You have logs of 1,000 agent failures. Design a pipeline to: analyze failure patterns, extract insights, and update the agent system (prompts, tools, or parameters) to avoid similar failures.
expected_approach: Use Error Analysis for failure pattern categorization, Theme Clustering for insight extraction from failure logs.

title: Complex System Orchestration with Agents
description: Design an agentic system for a complex workflow: research legal precedents → analyze case details → generate legal brief → present to client with confidence scores. Include agent roles, communication, and quality gates.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for agent roles and quality gates, Pipeline Design for orchestration flow.

title: Agent Behavior Prediction and Control
description: Given historical logs of agent behavior, design a method to predict what an agent will do next and intervene if needed. Use causal modeling or behavioral clustering to identify patterns.
expected_approach: Use Theme Clustering for behavioral pattern identification, Algorithm Deep Dive for causal modeling approach.

title: Emergent Behaviors in Multi-Agent Systems
description: When you deploy multiple agents with simple individual rules, complex emergent behaviors arise (cooperation, specialization, conflict). Design experiments to measure and control emergence. How do you prevent unintended behaviors?
expected_approach: Use A/B Test Design for emergence measurement experiments, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for analyzing results.

title: Agent Self-Improvement Without Retraining
description: Design an agent system that improves its own prompts, tools, or reasoning without retraining the underlying LLM. How do you ensure improvements generalize and don't overfit to specific tasks?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for self-improvement system, A/B Test Design for generalization validation.

title: Adversarial Robustness of Agents
description: An attacker can manipulate tool outputs or provide adversarial prompts. Design defenses: input validation, anomaly detection, fallback mechanisms. How do you measure robustness?
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for robustness measurement, Failure Mode Analysis for adversarial defense design.

title: Agent Curriculum Learning
description: Instead of random task order, design a curriculum where agents solve easier tasks first, building skills for harder tasks. Define task difficulty, skill dependencies, and progression criteria.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for curriculum progression, Evaluation Rubric Design for task difficulty and progression criteria.

title: Meta-Learning for Agent Prompting
description: Collect 100 tasks with their optimal prompts (found via grid search). Train a meta-learner to predict the best prompt for a new task based on task characteristics. How do you encode tasks and prompts for meta-learning?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for meta-learning encoding, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for optimal prompt analysis.

title: Collaborative Agent Frameworks
description: Design a framework for agents to collaborate on a complex problem (e.g., scientific research): roles (observer, experimenter, analyst), communication protocols, conflict resolution, and consensus mechanisms.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for collaborative framework, Pipeline Design for role and communication protocols.

title: Verifiable Reasoning in Agents
description: Design an agent system where all reasoning steps are verifiable by external validators. How do you generate proofs of reasoning? When is formal verification tractable vs intractable?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for verification methods, Formal Definition for tractability analysis.

title: Adaptive Agent Architectures
description: Design an agent system that adapts its own architecture per task: selects planning algorithm, memory type, tool set, and reasoning depth based on task properties. Formalize task properties and architecture decisions.
expected_approach: Use Design Space Exploration for architecture decision space, Architecture Pattern Catalog for per-task adaptation.

title: Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Agent Goals
description: Given logs of successful agent trajectories, infer the underlying reward function the agent optimizes for. How do you handle ambiguity (multiple reward functions explain data)? Use maximum entropy IRL or Bayesian approaches.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for IRL algorithms, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for trajectory analysis, Formal Definition for reward function formulation.

title: Real-Time Agent Monitoring and Adaptation
description: Build a system that monitors agent health metrics (success rate, cost per task, error frequency) in real-time and adapts agent behavior (parameter tuning, prompt modification, tool pool changes) based on performance.
expected_approach: Use Monitoring Design for health metrics, Pipeline Design for adaptation pipeline, Architecture Pattern Catalog for system structure.

title: Agent Knowledge Graph Construction
description: Design a system where agents build and maintain a knowledge graph of entities, relationships, and facts encountered during task solving. How do you update the graph, resolve conflicts, and query it for reasoning?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for knowledge graph design, Pipeline Design for graph maintenance, Multi-Source Integration for conflict resolution.

title: Scalable Agent Execution Framework
description: You need to run 100,000 agent tasks simultaneously. Design a distributed execution framework: task distribution, agent process management, result aggregation, fault tolerance, and cost optimization.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for distributed framework, Resource Estimation for capacity planning, Scalability Analysis for fault tolerance.

title: Human-in-the-Loop Agent Improvement
description: Design a pipeline for iteratively improving agents with human feedback: collect trajectories, identify suboptimal decisions, gather human corrections, update agent policy. Balance human effort with improvement gains.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for improvement pipeline, Human Evaluation Design for correction collection, Theme Clustering for suboptimal decision patterns.

title: Agent Capability Benchmarking
description: You want to systematically measure what agents can and cannot do. Design a benchmark suite covering breadth (20 task types), depth (3 difficulty levels), and novel tasks. Define success criteria and measure sample efficiency.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for benchmark suite, Evaluation Rubric Design for success criteria, Dataset and Benchmark Analysis for difficulty calibration.

title: Compositional Reasoning in Agents
description: Design an agent system that solves problems by composing learned skills from a skill library. How do you ensure skills compose correctly? Handle skill interactions and dependencies?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for skill composition, Testing Strategy for correctness validation, Cross-Domain Pattern Extraction for skill interactions.

title: Counterfactual Reasoning for Agent Improvement
description: Given an agent trajectory, generate counterfactuals (what if the agent made decision X instead of Y). Use counterfactuals to measure decision impact and train agents on contrastive examples.
expected_approach: Use Thought Experiment for counterfactual generation, Algorithm Deep Dive for impact measurement, A/B Test Design for contrastive training.

title: Privacy-Preserving Agent Collaboration
description: Multiple agents need to collaborate on tasks with sensitive data. Design privacy-preserving mechanisms: federated learning, differential privacy, secure multi-party computation. Trade-off privacy vs agent capability.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for privacy mechanisms, Scalability Analysis for privacy-capability tradeoff, Trade-off Analysis for mechanism selection.

title: Agent Fairness and Bias Detection
description: Design a system to detect and mitigate bias in agentic decisions. What biases can agents develop (historical data, tool bias, prompt bias)? How do you audit fairness and ensure equitable outcomes across demographics?
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for bias identification, Evaluation Rubric Design for fairness auditing, Architecture Pattern Catalog for mitigation system.

title: RAG Pipeline Fundamentals
description: Explain Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): how it retrieves relevant context before generation. Compare RAG to fine-tuning for knowledge injection. When is each better?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for RAG pipeline components, Comparative Technical Analysis for RAG vs fine-tuning.

title: Dense vs Sparse Retrieval
description: Compare dense retrieval (embeddings + nearest neighbor) and sparse retrieval (BM25, keyword search). When is each better? Can they be combined?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for dense vs sparse, Algorithm Deep Dive for embedding nearest neighbor mechanics.

title: Embedding Models for Retrieval
description: Explain what makes a good embedding model for retrieval. Describe MTEB benchmarks and metrics like nDCG and MRR. Name three popular embedding models.
expected_approach: Use Dataset and Benchmark Analysis for MTEB benchmarks, Metric Selection for nDCG/MRR explanation.

title: Query Expansion Techniques
description: Define query expansion: why it helps retrieval, and name three techniques (pseudo-relevance feedback, synonyms, semantic expansion). Give an example where expansion helps.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for query expansion techniques, Example Generation for concrete expansion example.

title: Vector Databases Basics
description: Explain vector databases (FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate). What indexing structures do they use (LSH, HNSW, IVF)? When would you use each?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for FAISS/Pinecone/Weaviate, Architecture Pattern Catalog for indexing structures.

title: Chunking Strategies for Documents
description: When indexing long documents for retrieval, chunking matters. Compare fixed-size chunking, sentence-level, and semantic chunking. What are the trade-offs?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for chunking strategies, Trade-off Analysis for granularity trade-offs.

title: Re-ranking and Cross-Encoders
description: Explain re-ranking: why retrieve many candidates (top-100) then re-rank to top-5? Describe cross-encoders vs bi-encoders. What's the computational trade-off?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for cross-encoder vs bi-encoder, Trade-off Analysis for computational trade-off.

title: Knowledge Graphs for Retrieval
description: How do knowledge graphs improve retrieval? Compare graph-based retrieval to embedding-based. When would you use each?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for graph vs embedding retrieval, Architecture Pattern Catalog for knowledge graph structure.

title: BM25 Algorithm Details
description: Explain BM25: the formula, parameters (k1, b), and why it works well for keyword matching. How does it compare to TF-IDF?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for BM25 formula and parameters, Formal Definition for mathematical comparison with TF-IDF.

title: Evaluation Metrics for Retrieval
description: Define nDCG (Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain), MRR (Mean Reciprocal Rank), and MAP (Mean Average Precision). Why measure each? Which is best for different scenarios?
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for nDCG/MRR/MAP explanation, Formal Definition for mathematical definitions.

title: Hybrid Search Best Practices
description: Hybrid search combines multiple retrieval methods (dense + sparse, embedding + keyword). Compare RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) vs learned combination weights.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for RRF vs learned weights, Architecture Pattern Catalog for hybrid search design.

title: Cold Start Problem in Retrieval
description: When first deploying a retrieval system, there's minimal interaction data. How do you bootstrap: content-based features, transfer learning, or human judgments?
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for cold start diagnosis, Gap Analysis for identifying what's missing and how to bootstrap.

title: Personalization in Retrieval
description: How do you personalize retrieval systems? Consider user history, preferences, and context. Compare collaborative filtering, content-based, and hybrid approaches.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for personalization approaches, Architecture Pattern Catalog for system design.

title: Retrieval Feedback Loops
description: Explain how user clicks, dwell time, and explicit feedback train retrieval models. What's the difference between implicit and explicit feedback? How do you handle position bias?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for feedback loop mechanics, Bias Detection for position bias handling.

title: Semantic Search Fundamentals
description: Explain semantic search: embedding both queries and documents, then finding nearest neighbors. Compare to keyword search. When is semantic better?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for semantic search mechanics, Comparative Technical Analysis for vs keyword search.

title: Advanced RAG Architecture
description: Design an advanced RAG system: multi-stage retrieval (retrieve docs → sentences → facts), adaptive retrieval (query-dependent top-k), and multi-hop reasoning where retrieved context triggers more retrievals.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for multi-stage RAG, Pipeline Design for multi-hop retrieval orchestration.

title: Embedding Space Analysis
description: You notice that your embedding model clusters documents by date instead of topic. Analyze why (temporal bias in training data). Design experiments to measure and correct this.
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for temporal bias diagnosis, A/B Test Design for correction experiments.

title: Query Expansion Strategy Design
description: Design a comprehensive query expansion system: analyze query intent (yes/no question vs fact-seeking), use pseudo-relevance feedback to identify related terms, and weight original vs expanded terms.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for expansion system, Algorithm Deep Dive for intent analysis and term weighting.

title: Vector Database Index Optimization
description: You're indexing 100M documents. Compare HNSW, IVF, and SCANN in terms of memory, search speed, and accuracy. Design an indexing strategy for your constraints.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for HNSW/IVF/SCANN, Resource Estimation for memory/speed constraints.

title: Semantic Chunking Strategy
description: Design a semantic chunking algorithm: detect natural boundaries in text (paragraph breaks, topic shifts, section headers) and chunk accordingly. How do you handle cross-chunk dependencies?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for semantic boundary detection, Pipeline Design for chunking algorithm.

title: Learned Ranking Pipeline
description: Design a learning-to-rank (LTR) system: feature engineering (query-doc relevance, retrieval scores, document quality), training data collection, and ranking model training. What model would you use?
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for LTR system, Algorithm Deep Dive for ranking model training.

title: Handling Retrieval Failures
description: Your retrieval system sometimes returns irrelevant context, degrading generation quality. Design detection mechanisms: relevance thresholding, consistency checking, or semantic similarity gating.
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for retrieval failure detection, System Debugging for recovery mechanisms.

title: Knowledge Graph Embedding for Retrieval
description: You have a knowledge graph (entities, relationships). Design embeddings that capture both entity semantics and relationship structure. Use techniques like TransE or GCN. How do you query the graph?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for TransE/GCN embeddings, Architecture Pattern Catalog for graph query system.

title: Multimodal Retrieval System
description: Design a retrieval system for multimodal data (text, images, tables). Use aligned embeddings (CLIP-style) that project different modalities into a shared space. How do you handle modality-specific features?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for multimodal retrieval design, Algorithm Deep Dive for CLIP-style alignment.

title: Temporal Awareness in Retrieval
description: Retrieval systems should prefer recent documents but also value classics. Design a temporal weighting scheme: decay old documents but allow high-quality classics to rank highly.
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for recency vs quality balance, Algorithm Deep Dive for temporal weighting scheme.

title: Domain Adaptation for Embeddings
description: You're using a general-purpose embedding model (trained on web data) but need to retrieve scientific papers. Design a domain adaptation pipeline: fine-tune embeddings on in-domain data, evaluate on domain-specific benchmarks.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for domain adaptation, Benchmark Protocol Design for domain-specific evaluation.

title: Retrieval at Scale with Constraints
description: Design retrieval for 1B documents with <100ms latency and <1GB memory per server. What indexing strategy, quantization, and distribution would you use?
expected_approach: Use Resource Estimation for capacity constraints, Scalability Analysis for distribution and quantization strategy.

title: Diversity in Retrieval Results
description: Simple retrieval often returns similar documents (all discussing the same aspect). Design diversity-aware retrieval: measure diversity, select diverse subsets (maximal marginal relevance, determinantal point processes).
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for MMR/DPP, Trade-off Analysis for diversity vs relevance.

title: Retrieval for Long-Form Generation
description: For generating long documents (research paper, novel), simple retrieval doesn't work — need multiple relevant passages from different sources. Design a multi-document retrieval system.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for multi-document retrieval, Multi-Source Integration for passage combination.

title: Cross-Lingual Retrieval
description: Design a retrieval system that finds relevant documents across languages. Use multilingual embeddings (mE5, mBERT) or translation. Compare approaches.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for multilingual approaches, Algorithm Deep Dive for cross-lingual embeddings.

title: End-to-End RAG System Optimization
description: You're building a RAG system for customer support: retrieve relevant docs → generate answer. Optimize the entire pipeline: retrieval speed, generation latency, accuracy. Measure end-to-end performance.
expected_approach: Use Performance Profiling for pipeline optimization, Pipeline Design for end-to-end RAG system.

title: Adaptive Retrieval based on Query Complexity
description: Simple queries need simple retrieval, complex queries need multi-hop reasoning. Design a system that estimates query complexity and adapts retrieval accordingly (depth, number of hops, reranking rounds).
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for adaptive system design, Algorithm Deep Dive for query complexity estimation.

title: Retrieval with Explanations
description: Design a retrieval system that not only ranks documents but also explains why each is relevant. Generate explanations (query-doc similarity, keyword overlap, semantic connection). Validate with human judgment.
expected_approach: Use Visualization Design for explanation generation, Human Evaluation Design for explanation validation.

title: Handling Contradictory Retrieved Information
description: Retrieved documents sometimes contradict each other. Design a system to detect contradictions and synthesize conflicting information. When should the generator merge viewpoints vs ask for clarification?
expected_approach: Use Multi-Source Integration for contradiction detection, Assumption Surfacing for conflict resolution strategy.

title: Continual Learning for Retrieval
description: A retrieval system in production encounters new documents daily and user feedback on relevance judgments. Design a continual learning pipeline: update embeddings, rerank models, without full retraining.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for continual learning system, Algorithm Deep Dive for incremental embedding updates.

title: Retrieval for Code Search
description: Design a retrieval system for code repositories: handle code-specific structure (functions, classes, patterns). Compare text retrieval, AST-based retrieval, and semantic code embeddings. Evaluate on code search benchmarks.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for code retrieval approaches, Benchmark Protocol Design for code search evaluation.

title: Privacy-Preserving Dense Retrieval
description: Design a dense retrieval system where queries and documents are encrypted (using secure multiparty computation or homomorphic encryption). Measure the privacy-utility tradeoff.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for homomorphic encryption/MPC, Trade-off Analysis for privacy-utility measurement.

title: Retrieval with Causal Reasoning
description: Some queries require causal reasoning (why did X happen), not just relevance matching. Design a causal retrieval system using causal graphs or counterfactual reasoning.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for causal graph mechanics, Thought Experiment for counterfactual reasoning design.

title: Dynamic Weighting of Retrieval Dimensions
description: Retrieval systems typically use static weights (0.5*relevance + 0.3*freshness + 0.2*popularity). Design a system that learns query-specific weights using Thompson Sampling or reinforcement learning.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for Thompson Sampling/RL weight learning, A/B Test Design for dynamic vs static weight comparison.

title: Evaluating Retrieval System Robustness
description: Design a robustness evaluation framework for retrieval systems: adversarial queries, distribution shift, adversarial documents. Measure performance degradation and design defenses.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for robustness evaluation framework, Failure Mode Analysis for degradation analysis.

title: Meta-Learning for Retrieval Hyperparameters
description: Retrieval systems have many hyperparameters (weights, chunk size, rerank cutoff). Collect a dataset of (task_metadata, optimal_hyperparameters) and train a meta-learner to predict hyperparameters for new tasks.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for meta-learning, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for hyperparameter dataset, Design Space Exploration for task encoding.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Fine-Tuning
description: Instead of fine-tuning a model directly, use retrieval during fine-tuning to inject task-relevant context. Design a curriculum: early epochs use broad retrieval, later epochs use targeted retrieval.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for retrieval-augmented fine-tuning, Algorithm Deep Dive for curriculum design, A/B Test for broad vs targeted retrieval.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Retrieval
description: Design a GNN-based retrieval system where documents are nodes, edges represent citations/references. Use GNN to propagate relevance signals through the graph. How does performance compare to non-graph retrieval?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for GNN mechanics, Architecture Pattern Catalog for graph retrieval design, Benchmark Protocol for performance comparison.

title: Retrieval System Fairness and Bias
description: Analyze potential biases in retrieval systems: does the system retrieve documents about certain demographics less frequently? Design fairness metrics and mitigation strategies.
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for retrieval bias analysis, Metric Selection for fairness metrics, Architecture Pattern Catalog for mitigation design.

title: Zero-Shot Retrieval Generalization
description: Train a retrieval system on one domain (news articles), then test on a new domain (scientific papers) without fine-tuning. Measure generalization. Design domain-agnostic retrieval architectures.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for generalization testing, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for cross-domain results, Algorithm Deep Dive for domain-agnostic design.

title: Instruction-Tuned Retrieval Models
description: Design a retrieval system where both queries and documents are conditioned on task instructions (e.g., 'Find documents relevant for summarization' vs 'Find documents relevant for question answering'). Train instruction-tuned embeddings.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for instruction-tuned embeddings, Architecture Pattern Catalog for conditioned retrieval, A/B Test for instruction comparison.

title: Retrieval for Fact Verification
description: Design a retrieval system that finds evidence to support/refute claims. Compare retrieval for evidence vs retrieval for general relevance. How do you validate retrieved facts?
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for fact verification retrieval, Benchmark Protocol for evidence evaluation, Assumption Surfacing for fact validation.

title: Efficient Dense Retrieval for Mobile
description: Design a dense retrieval system that runs on mobile devices (limited memory, CPU). Use quantization, distillation, and lightweight embeddings. Trade accuracy for efficiency.
expected_approach: Use Performance Profiling for mobile optimization, Resource Estimation for mobile constraints, Algorithm Deep Dive for quantization/distillation.

title: Retrieval Interaction Effects
description: Some queries have synergistic interactions (query A + query B together retrieve better results than separately). Design a system that detects and exploits query interactions.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for interaction detection, A/B Test for synergy measurement, Cross-Domain Pattern Extraction for interaction exploitation.

title: Benchmark Development for Advanced Retrieval
description: Design a benchmark that goes beyond keyword matching: requires semantic understanding, multi-hop reasoning, temporal reasoning, and real-world difficulty. Collect or generate 500+ examples, collect human annotations, and establish baselines.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for benchmark design, Human Evaluation Design for annotation collection, Dataset and Benchmark Analysis for baseline establishment.

title: Agent Roles and Specialization
description: In a multi-agent system, agents can have different roles (researcher, synthesizer, critic). Explain how role specialization improves collective performance. Give one example.
expected_approach: Use Taxonomy for role classification, Example Generation for specialization example.

title: Agent Communication Protocols
description: How do agents communicate in multi-agent systems? Compare message passing, publish-subscribe, and shared memory. When is each appropriate?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for communication protocols, Architecture Pattern Catalog for system design.

title: Task Allocation in Multi-Agent Systems
description: How do agents decide who does what task? Compare centralized assignment, decentralized negotiation, and market-based allocation. What are trade-offs?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for allocation strategies, Trade-off Analysis for centralized vs decentralized trade-offs.

title: Coordination Mechanisms
description: Name three coordination mechanisms in multi-agent systems (blackboard architecture, contract net, auction). Explain when each is useful.
expected_approach: Use Taxonomy for coordination mechanism classification, Example Generation for concrete mechanism examples.

title: Consensus Algorithms
description: How do agents reach consensus on decisions? Compare majority voting, weighted voting, and Byzantine fault-tolerant algorithms. When is each used?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for consensus algorithms, Algorithm Deep Dive for Byzantine fault tolerance.

title: Conflict Resolution in Teams
description: When agents disagree, how do they resolve conflicts? Compare voting, negotiation, hierarchical override. When would each be appropriate?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for conflict resolution approaches, Trade-off Analysis for approach selection.

title: Agent Scalability
description: How does multi-agent performance scale with the number of agents? Compare O(1), O(log n), O(n), and O(n²) communication protocols.
expected_approach: Use Scalability and Complexity Analysis for communication scaling, Comparative Technical Analysis for protocol comparison.

title: Emergent Behavior
description: What is emergent behavior in multi-agent systems? Give two examples where simple individual rules lead to complex collective behavior.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for emergence mechanics, Example Generation for two concrete emergence examples.

title: Agent Learning from Interactions
description: How can agents improve by learning from their interactions with other agents? Compare centralized learning, federated learning, and decentralized learning.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for learning approaches, Algorithm Deep Dive for federated/decentralized learning.

title: Debate Framework for Multi-Agent Reasoning
description: In a debate framework, agents argue for/against propositions. Explain how debate can improve reasoning compared to single agents. Give a concrete example.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for debate framework mechanics, Example Generation for concrete debate example.

title: Hierarchical vs Flat Organization
description: Compare hierarchical (manager → workers) vs flat (all equal) agent organizations. What are the trade-offs in communication, responsiveness, and robustness?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for hierarchical vs flat, Trade-off Analysis for communication/responsiveness/robustness.

title: Shared Knowledge Bases
description: Agents working together often maintain shared knowledge. Design a system for maintaining consistency when multiple agents update knowledge simultaneously.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for shared knowledge system, Pipeline Design for consistency maintenance.

title: Agent Accountability
description: In multi-agent systems, how do you track which agent made which decision? Design an accountability mechanism that enables auditing and debugging.
expected_approach: Use Monitoring and Observability for audit trail design, Architecture Pattern Catalog for accountability mechanism.

title: Advanced Coordination Protocol Design
description: Design a coordination protocol for 10 agents solving a complex task: define message types, decision points, and error handling. How do you ensure no agent blocks progress?
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for coordination protocol, Architecture Pattern Catalog for message types and error handling.

title: Task Allocation Optimization
description: You have 5 agents with different skills and 20 tasks with different requirements. Formalize the allocation problem (knapsack-style) and design an algorithm to assign tasks optimally.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for optimization formulation, Resource Estimation for task-skill matching.

title: Heterogeneous Agent Teams
description: Design a team of heterogeneous agents (different capabilities, knowledge, speeds). How does diversity help or hurt? Design team composition for complex tasks.
expected_approach: Use Design Space Exploration for team composition, Comparative Technical Analysis for diversity effects.

title: Communication Bandwidth Constraints
description: Agents have limited bandwidth to communicate. Design efficient communication protocols: what information is critical, what can be approximated, and how do you handle information loss?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for information priority, Pipeline Design for efficient communication protocol.

title: Handling Agent Failures
description: In a team of 5 agents, one becomes unavailable. Design mechanisms to detect failures, reassign tasks, and maintain progress without restarting.
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for failure detection, System Debugging for task reassignment and recovery.

title: Multi-Agent Planning Algorithms
description: Design a planning algorithm where agents collectively plan how to solve a complex problem. Compare centralized planning, decentralized planning, and iterative refinement.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for planning approaches, Algorithm Deep Dive for decentralized planning algorithms.

title: Debate and Voting for Accuracy
description: You want to use agent debate to improve decision quality. Design the debate format: what information agents share, how many rounds, how voting works, and how to detect collusion.
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for debate format criteria, Algorithm Deep Dive for voting and collusion detection.

title: Incentive Design for Cooperation
description: Design incentives (rewards, penalties) that encourage agents to cooperate rather than act selfishly. Analyze game-theoretic stability: will agents maintain cooperation?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for game-theoretic incentive design, Trade-off Analysis for cooperation stability.

title: Knowledge Synthesis from Multiple Agents
description: Multiple agents provide partial information (each observes different aspects). Design a synthesis mechanism to merge observations into a complete picture, handling contradictions.
expected_approach: Use Multi-Source Integration for observation merging, Assumption Surfacing for contradiction handling.

title: Multi-Agent Goal Alignment
description: Agents may have conflicting goals. Design a framework for aligning individual goals with team objectives. When should you compromise individual vs team goals?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for individual vs team goal balance, Architecture Pattern Catalog for alignment framework.

title: Emergent Specialization
description: In a team without explicit role assignment, agents naturally specialize (some become researchers, others synthesizers). Design mechanisms to encourage beneficial specialization.
expected_approach: Use Scenario Planning for specialization emergence, Algorithm Deep Dive for beneficial specialization mechanisms.

title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
description: Design a multi-agent RL system where agents learn policies that coordinate without central authority. Handle non-stationarity (other agents' policies changing). Compare centralized vs decentralized training.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for MARL algorithms, Comparative Technical Analysis for centralized vs decentralized training.

title: Distributed Data Processing with Agents
description: Design a system where agents process data chunks in parallel, then aggregate results. Handle skewed data distribution, straggler agents, and result merging.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for distributed processing, Resource Estimation for data distribution and straggler handling.

title: Trust and Reputation in Agent Teams
description: Design a trust system for agents: track reputation based on accuracy/reliability, use trust to weight agent contributions, and update trust over time.
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for trust metrics, Algorithm Deep Dive for reputation update mechanisms.

title: Optimization for Multi-Agent Systems
description: Design an optimization algorithm for multi-agent tasks: each agent's objective affects others. Compare federated optimization, gradient sharing, and consensus algorithms.
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for optimization approaches, Algorithm Deep Dive for federated optimization.

title: Scalable Multi-Agent Coordination
description: Design coordination for 1,000 agents. Traditional protocols have O(n²) communication. Design scalable alternatives using hierarchies, gossip, or approximations.
expected_approach: Use Scalability Analysis for 1,000-agent communication, Architecture Pattern Catalog for hierarchical/gossip design.

title: Testing and Debugging Multi-Agent Systems
description: Multi-agent systems are hard to test (non-determinism, race conditions, emergent behaviors). Design a testing framework: reproducibility, scenario generation, and bug detection.
expected_approach: Use Testing Strategy Design for multi-agent testing, System Debugging for non-determinism and race condition handling.

title: Large-Scale Coordination Protocol Design
description: Design a coordination protocol for 100+ agents working on a complex, long-running task. Include deadlock detection, failure recovery, and progress guarantees. Validate with simulations.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for large-scale protocol, Architecture Pattern Catalog for deadlock detection, A/B Test for simulation validation.

title: Emergent Strategy Formation
description: Agents learn strategies through interaction. Design mechanisms to measure and encourage emergence of sophisticated strategies (e.g., deception, alliance formation, niche specialization).
expected_approach: Use Scenario Planning for strategy emergence, A/B Test Design for strategy measurement experiments.

title: Agent Heterogeneity and Diversity
description: Analyze how diverse agent teams (different architectures, knowledge, reasoning styles) outperform homogeneous teams. Design diversity metrics and selection algorithms.
expected_approach: Use Empirical Evidence Synthesis for diversity analysis, Metric Selection for diversity metrics.

title: Byzantine-Tolerant Multi-Agent Systems
description: Design a system where some agents are adversarial (Byzantine). Use Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus algorithms. How many adversarial agents can you tolerate?
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for Byzantine fault tolerance, Scalability Analysis for adversarial agent tolerance bounds.

title: Optimal Agent Debate Format
description: Design an optimal debate format for 3 agents: Who speaks when? How is information shared? How is consensus determined? Use game theory and information theory to justify design.
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for debate format criteria, Algorithm Deep Dive for game-theoretic justification.

title: Multi-Agent System Dynamics Modeling
description: Model multi-agent system dynamics as differential equations (agent populations, knowledge diffusion, cooperation levels). Analyze stability, equilibria, and transient behavior.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for differential equation modeling, Formal Definition for equilibria formalization.

title: Information Aggregation in Prediction Markets
description: Design a prediction market where agents trade contracts based on beliefs about outcomes. Analyze whether market prices aggregate information efficiently. Compare to voting and other aggregation methods.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for prediction market mechanics, Comparative Technical Analysis for vs voting/aggregation.

title: Multi-Agent Inverse Reinforcement Learning
description: Given multi-agent trajectories, infer the reward functions and constraints each agent optimizes. Handle partial observability and agent interdependencies.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for multi-agent IRL, Formal Definition for reward function and interdependency formalization.

title: Learning Communication Protocols
description: Design a system where agents learn to communicate efficiently without predefined protocols. Use emergent communication or multi-agent reinforcement learning. Measure protocol interpretability.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for emergent communication, Metric Selection for protocol interpretability metrics.

title: Adaptive Organization Structure
description: Design a system where agents dynamically reorganize (hierarchy ↔ flat ↔ clusters) based on task demands. When is reorganization beneficial? Measure reorganization cost vs benefit.
expected_approach: Use Design Space Exploration for organization structure options, Trade-off Analysis for reorganization cost vs benefit.

title: Fairness in Multi-Agent Outcomes
description: Design fairness mechanisms for multi-agent systems: ensure all agents benefit from cooperation, prevent exploitation, and handle distributional fairness. Formalize and measure fairness.
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for fairness analysis, Formal Definition for fairness formalization, Trade-off Analysis for distributional fairness.

title: Multi-Agent System Verification
description: Design formal verification techniques for multi-agent systems: safety properties (agents never reach bad states), liveness properties (agents make progress), and robustness.
expected_approach: Use Formal Definition for safety/liveness properties, Testing Strategy for verification approach, Algorithm Deep Dive for formal methods.

title: Scalable Knowledge Sharing
description: Design a knowledge-sharing system for 1,000 agents: efficient representation, query mechanisms, consistency maintenance, and privacy preservation. Measure communication overhead.
expected_approach: Use Architecture Pattern Catalog for knowledge sharing design, Scalability Analysis for communication overhead, Pipeline Design for consistency.

title: Multi-Agent Planning with Uncertainty
description: Agents plan collaboratively but face uncertain outcomes (actions fail, observations are noisy). Design robust planning: contingency planning, reactive replanning, and uncertainty propagation.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for robust planning, Scenario Planning for contingency design, Pipeline Design for reactive replanning.

title: Agent Meta-Learning for Collaboration
description: Train agents to collaborate with new teammates they've never met. Use meta-learning to develop adaptable collaboration strategies. Test on novel team compositions.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for collaboration meta-learning, A/B Test for novel team testing, Cross-Domain Pattern for adaptable strategies.

title: Conflict-Free Multi-Agent Consensus
description: Design consensus algorithms that always converge, even with Byzantine agents and incomplete information. Analyze convergence guarantees and communication complexity.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for consensus algorithms, Formal Definition for convergence guarantees, Scalability Analysis for communication complexity.

title: Measuring Collective Intelligence
description: Design metrics for collective intelligence: wisdom of crowds, diversity bonus, coordination efficiency. Compare agent teams to centralized systems and human teams.
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for collective intelligence metrics, Benchmark Protocol for comparison framework, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for analysis.

title: Incentive Compatibility in Multi-Agent Design
description: Design multi-agent systems that are incentive-compatible: agents honestly report information and follow protocol without manipulation. Use game theory, mechanism design.
expected_approach: Use Algorithm Deep Dive for mechanism design, Formal Definition for incentive compatibility, A/B Test for manipulation resistance.

title: Temporal Coordination in Multi-Agent Systems
description: Design temporal coordination when agents have different speeds, communication latencies, and timing constraints. Handle synchronization, deadline coordination, and time-sensitive decisions.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for temporal coordination, Algorithm Deep Dive for synchronization mechanisms, Scalability Analysis for timing constraints.

title: Long-Horizon Multi-Agent Task Execution
description: Design a system where agents collaboratively solve a complex, long-duration task (days or weeks). Include progress tracking, milestone planning, role evolution, and recovery from disruptions.
expected_approach: Use Pipeline Design for long-horizon orchestration, Architecture Pattern Catalog for role evolution, Monitoring Design for progress tracking.

title: LLM-as-Judge for Text Quality
description: Explain how to use an LLM as a judge for evaluating text quality. Describe the rubric, prompt design, and how to reduce judge bias. What are failure modes?
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for LLM judge rubric and prompt, Failure Mode Analysis for judge bias failure modes.

title: Standard Benchmarks Overview
description: Name five standard ML benchmarks: MMLU, HumanEval, SQuAD, ImageNet, and one other. Briefly describe what each measures.
expected_approach: Use Dataset and Benchmark Analysis for benchmark descriptions, Taxonomy for benchmark classification.

title: Human Evaluation Protocols
description: Design a human evaluation protocol: sampling strategy, annotator instructions, quality control, and inter-annotator agreement measurement.
expected_approach: Use Human Evaluation Design for protocol structure, Evaluation Rubric Design for annotator instructions.

title: Inter-Annotator Agreement
description: Define Cohen's Kappa, Fleiss' Kappa, and Krippendorff's Alpha. When is each appropriate? How do you interpret agreement levels?
expected_approach: Use Statistical Significance Testing for agreement metrics, Formal Definition for Kappa/Alpha formulas.

title: Automatic Metrics for NLP
description: Explain BLEU, ROUGE, and METEOR. What do they measure and why? What are their limitations?
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for BLEU/ROUGE/METEOR explanation, Comparative Technical Analysis for limitations comparison.

title: Bias Detection in ML Systems
description: Describe methods for detecting bias in ML systems: stratified analysis, demographic parity, equalized odds. When would you use each?
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for bias identification methods, Metric Selection for demographic parity and equalized odds.

title: Robustness Testing
description: Explain robustness testing: adversarial examples, distribution shift, out-of-distribution data. Design a robustness test for an image classifier.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for robustness test design, Example Generation for image classifier test examples.

title: Calibration and Uncertainty
description: Explain calibration: when a model says 90% confidence, it should be right ~90% of the time. How do you measure calibration? How do you improve it?
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for calibration metrics, Algorithm Deep Dive for calibration improvement methods.

title: A/B Testing for ML Models
description: Design an A/B test to compare two model versions. How do you determine sample size, randomization, and statistical significance?
expected_approach: Use A/B Test Design for model comparison, Statistical Significance Testing for sample size and significance.

title: Fairness Metrics and Trade-offs
description: Define fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds, predictive parity). Explain why different metrics can conflict. How do you choose?
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for fairness metric definitions, Trade-off Analysis for metric conflicts.

title: Interpretability and Explainability
description: Distinguish interpretability (is the model transparent) from explainability (can you explain decisions). Name three explainability techniques.
expected_approach: Use Taxonomy for interpretability vs explainability distinction, Example Generation for three techniques.

title: Reward Hacking Prevention
description: Explain reward hacking: when agents optimize the metric instead of the true objective. Give two examples. How do you prevent it?
expected_approach: Use Failure Mode Analysis for reward hacking mechanisms, Example Generation for concrete hacking examples.

title: Cross-Validation Strategies
description: Compare k-fold, stratified k-fold, and time-series cross-validation strategies.
