For each, explain the assumptions about data distribution, when the strategy is appropriate (e.g., class imbalance, temporal dependencies), and common pitfalls like data leakage in time-series splits. When is each the right choice?
expected_approach: Use Comparative Technical Analysis for cross-validation strategies, Statistical Significance Testing for data leakage pitfalls.

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Explain p-values, confidence intervals, and power analysis. Design a significance test for comparing two models.
expected_approach: Use Statistical Significance Testing for p-values and power analysis, Formal Definition for mathematical formulation.

title: Advanced LLM Judging Design
description: Design a sophisticated LLM-as-judge system: multi-dimensional evaluation (fluency, relevance, factuality), chain-of-thought judging, confidence scores, and bias mitigation.
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for multi-dimensional judge system, Bias Detection for bias mitigation in judging.

title: Custom Benchmark Design
description: Design a custom benchmark for evaluating agents on reasoning tasks: define difficulty levels, include diverse task types (logic, math, language), collect gold-standard annotations.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for benchmark structure, Evaluation Rubric Design for difficulty levels and annotations.

title: Scale Human Evaluation
description: You need to annotate 10,000 examples but human annotation is expensive. Design a strategy: sampling, crowdsourcing, expert/non-expert splits, quality control thresholds.
expected_approach: Use Human Evaluation Design for annotation strategy, Resource Estimation for crowdsourcing cost planning.

title: Measuring Hallucination in LLMs
description: Design metrics and methods to measure hallucination (when LLMs generate false information). Compare factuality checking, consistency scores, and semantic entailment.
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for hallucination metrics, Comparative Technical Analysis for detection method comparison.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for Metrics
description: A model's performance depends on evaluation metric choice (BLEU vs METEOR for translation). Design a sensitivity analysis: how much do rankings change between metrics?
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for metric sensitivity analysis, Statistical Significance Testing for ranking stability.

title: Temporal Drift Detection
description: A deployed model's performance degrades over time (data distribution shifts). Design a drift detection system: metrics, thresholds, and retraining triggers.
expected_approach: Use Monitoring and Observability for drift detection system, Metric Selection for drift metrics and thresholds.

title: Adversarial Robustness Benchmarking
description: Design a benchmark for adversarial robustness: adversarial examples (FGSM, PGD), backdoor attacks, and poisoning. Measure both robustness and clean accuracy.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for adversarial robustness benchmark, Bias Detection for robustness vs clean accuracy balance.

title: Fairness in Multi-Class Classification
description: You have 10 output classes and 5 demographic groups. Design fairness metrics and mitigation: which fairness constraint (group fairness, individual fairness, causal fairness)?
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for multi-class fairness metrics, Metric Selection for constraint selection.

title: Error Analysis Methodology
description: Design a systematic error analysis pipeline: categorize errors (false positives, false negatives), root cause analysis, and failure mode taxonomy.
expected_approach: Use Error Analysis for systematic categorization, Root Cause Analysis for failure mode taxonomy.

title: Measuring Agent Efficiency
description: Design metrics for agent efficiency: tokens used, steps taken, wall-clock time, compute cost. How do you compare efficiency across tasks of varying difficulty?
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for efficiency metrics, Comparative Technical Analysis for cross-difficulty comparison.

title: Confounding Variable Analysis
description: Model A outperforms Model B on your benchmark. Design an analysis to check if B's poor performance is due to the modeling approach or confounding factors (data quality, hyperparameter tuning).
expected_approach: Use A/B Test Design for confounding factor analysis, Assumption Surfacing for identifying hidden confounders.

title: Few-Shot Learning Evaluation
description: Design an evaluation protocol for few-shot learning: vary sample sizes, measure learning curves, compare zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot performance.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for few-shot evaluation protocol, A/B Test Design for sample size variation.

title: Long-Form Generation Evaluation
description: Evaluating long documents (essays, reports) with short metrics (BLEU) fails. Design comprehensive evaluation: structure, coherence, factuality, relevance. Combine automatic and human judgment.
expected_approach: Use Evaluation Rubric Design for long-form criteria, Human Evaluation Design for combined judgment protocol.

title: Negative Results and Publication Bias
description: Analyze publication bias: positive results are published, negative results disappear. Design a process to scientifically document negative results and ensure fair statistical reporting.
expected_approach: Use Research Gap Identification for identifying suppressed negative results, Reproducibility Assessment for fair statistical reporting.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization Evaluation
description: You want to optimize multiple objectives (accuracy, fairness, latency). Design evaluation: Pareto frontier analysis, trade-off visualization, and multi-objective metrics.
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for Pareto frontier, Visualization Design for multi-objective visualization.

title: Evaluation in Domain Shift
description: Model trained on ImageNet is deployed on medical images. Design evaluation for domain shift: in-distribution vs out-of-distribution performance, adaptation methods.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol Design for domain shift evaluation, A/B Test Design for in-vs-out-distribution comparison.

title: Comprehensive LLM Evaluation Framework
description: Design an end-to-end evaluation framework for LLMs: multiple tasks (reasoning, knowledge, language, code), diverse metrics (accuracy, efficiency, robustness), bias detection. Create taxonomy of capabilities.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for framework structure, Evaluation Rubric for capability criteria, Taxonomy for capability classification.

title: Designing Unbiased Evaluation Benchmarks
description: Design a benchmark that resists gaming and cheating: diverse examples, no data leakage, hard negatives, adversarial examples. Validate that solutions must understand the problem, not memorize.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for unbiased benchmark design, Bias Detection for gaming and data leakage prevention.

title: Human-LLM Evaluation Agreement
description: Investigate disagreement between human and LLM judgments: statistical analysis, error patterns, sources of disagreement. When should you trust human vs LLM judgment?
expected_approach: Use Human Evaluation Design for agreement study, Statistical Significance Testing for disagreement analysis.

title: Scalable Fairness Auditing
description: Audit fairness of a recommender system with billions of users/items: sampling strategies, group representation, fairness metrics, and interventions. Measure fairness violations post-hoc.
expected_approach: Use Bias Detection for fairness auditing, Resource Estimation for sampling strategy at scale.

title: Causal Evaluation of Model Changes
description: You changed a model and metrics improved. Was it because of your change or confounding factors? Design causal inference methods: randomized trials, difference-in-differences, propensity score matching.
expected_approach: Use A/B Test Design for causal inference, Statistical Significance Testing for difference-in-differences analysis.

title: Detecting Shortcuts and Spurious Correlations
description: A model achieves high accuracy but uses spurious correlations (e.g., background patterns instead of objects). Design detection methods: saliency maps, causal analysis, adversarial tests.
expected_approach: Use Error Analysis for shortcut detection, Bias Detection for spurious correlation identification.

title: Metric Aggregation and Ranking
description: You have 15 different evaluation metrics. Design a principled approach to aggregate them into a single ranking. Handle metric weights, normalize scales, and detect conflicts.
expected_approach: Use Metric Selection for aggregation approach, Trade-off Analysis for metric weighting and conflict resolution.

title: Generalization Assessment
description: Test if a model truly generalizes or just memorizes. Design evaluation: hold-out test sets, domain shift tests, adversarial examples, and extrapolation tasks beyond training distribution.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for generalization tests, A/B Test Design for extrapolation evaluation.

title: Cost-Benefit Analysis of Evaluation
description: Comprehensive evaluation is expensive (annotation, compute, time). Design a cost-benefit analysis: which evaluation methods are worth their cost? When can you use cheaper proxies?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for evaluation cost-benefit, Resource Estimation for cheaper proxy identification.

title: Competitive Benchmarking Analysis
description: You have leaderboard rankings from 50 models. Analyze: what makes top models succeed (architectural choices, training data, hyperparameters)? Design a meta-analysis to extract insights.
expected_approach: Use Empirical Evidence Synthesis for leaderboard meta-analysis, Theme Clustering for success pattern extraction.

title: Interpretability-Performance Trade-off
description: More interpretable models (trees, linear) often have lower accuracy than black-box models (neural networks). Design evaluation to measure this trade-off. When is interpretability worth accuracy loss?
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for interpretability-performance frontier, Metric Selection for measurement approach, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for evidence.

title: Stress Testing ML Systems
description: Design comprehensive stress tests for deployed ML systems: adversarial inputs, noise injection, resource constraints, latency variations, and cascading failures.
expected_approach: Use Testing Strategy for stress test design, Benchmark Protocol for adversarial benchmarks, Failure Mode Analysis for cascading failures.

title: Meta-Analysis of Evaluation Methods
description: Compare different evaluation methods (LLM-as-judge vs human annotation vs automatic metrics) on the same 1,000 examples. Measure agreement, cost, and correlation with real-world outcomes.
expected_approach: Use Empirical Evidence Synthesis for method comparison, Human Evaluation Design for agreement study, Metric Selection for correlation metrics.

title: Temporal Evaluation for Continual Learning
description: A model learns continuously over months, encountering new data and tasks. Design evaluation: learning curves, forgetting measurements, forward/backward transfer, task interference.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for temporal evaluation, Metric Selection for forgetting/transfer metrics, Historical Evolution Analysis for learning trajectory.

title: Reproducibility and Replicability
description: Design a framework for reproducibility: code/data release, hyperparameter documentation, random seed control. Measure how many results replicate independently. Analyze failure modes.
expected_approach: Use Reproducibility Assessment for framework design, Documentation and Runbook for release protocol, Statistical Significance for replication analysis.

title: Backdoor Detection and Evaluation
description: Design evaluation to detect backdoor attacks in models: trigger patterns, targeted misclassification, and verification. Create adversarial benchmarks for backdoor robustness.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for backdoor evaluation, Failure Mode Analysis for attack detection, Bias Detection for targeted misclassification.

title: Privacy Preservation in Evaluation
description: Evaluate a model without revealing sensitive data: differential privacy for evaluation, federated evaluation, and secure computation. Measure privacy-accuracy trade-off.
expected_approach: Use Trade-off Analysis for privacy-accuracy trade-off, Algorithm Deep Dive for differential privacy, A/B Test for federated evaluation.

title: Sample Efficiency Evaluation
description: Design evaluation to measure sample efficiency: learning curves with varying data sizes, extrapolation to infinite data, comparison of data efficiency across models.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for sample efficiency evaluation, A/B Test Design for data size variation, Empirical Evidence Synthesis for cross-model comparison.

title: Axiom-Based Evaluation
description: Define axioms that models should satisfy (consistency, transitivity, monotonicity). Design tests to verify axioms. When do models violate axioms? Can you enforce them?
expected_approach: Use Formal Definition for axiom formulation, Benchmark Protocol for axiom testing, Testing Strategy for violation detection.

title: Comprehensive Research System Evaluation
description: Design a complete evaluation framework for a complex research system: task decomposition, independent component testing, end-to-end integration testing, failure mode analysis, comparison with baselines, and statistical reporting with effect sizes and confidence intervals.
expected_approach: Use Benchmark Protocol for evaluation framework, Ablation Study Design for component testing, Error Analysis for failure mode analysis.
